text: We used a three-port technique to explore the left thorax.
Pulmonary decortication was done using a lab.
Next step was dissection of posterior metastinum to look like the esophagus.
We passed an NG tube and again we made a methylene blue test in order to identify the site of the esophageal perforation.
we found a long esophageal rupture with mucous exposed.
The T-tube was inserted in the esophageal hole.
We perform a final pleural irrigation and insert two chest drains.
Post-operative esophagus X-rays show the T-tube in correct position without contrast leaks
Clinical progress was good with no leaks and no other symptoms.
